tags.evidence: grief and praise are inseparable
return of anyone who has been in the underworld
Grief is not the failure of love
in the presence of what you love
A metal roof lasts twenty years
recognized the enclosure of the occasion and refused it
It was an infrastructure for the return
The relationship is replaced with nothing
sidewalk equivalent of the speaking circle
The baker knows when someone on the block is in trouble
The intelligence was distributed
ten thousand low-stakes encounters that build
owned by no one
Nobody designed it. It emerged from millions
it belongs to no one and everyone
Like language itself
out of the underworld, toward the community
The direction of this chapter is outward and upward
learn to welcome and attend
People gathered in Zuccotti Park
govern themselves in public, without leaders
something simple and strange
A herd of centaurs occupied Wall Street
The mycorrhizal network, finally fast enough to see
open protocols that Berners-Lee gave away
distributed intelligence that none of them could have accessed alone
it needs to exodus from the subjective identities
they had discovered how to want together
The people's mic, the general assembly
discovered something previous left politics had missed
The herd needed governance
demolished from outside, by coordinated municipal action
the same pattern Federici documented
It destroys the conditions that make commons governance possible
anti-LGBTQ brewery seeking a permit next door
return nothing that cost anything
processing community energy into institutional legitimacy
But I burned out watching the institution
function exactly as designed
unblocked desiring production
the form that could hold that flow across time
the structure must carry the weight
The initiated one runs on gift
navigated as threat or as information
they have discovered what they carry
A herd of centaurs, each one carrying the oblique angle
The multitude itself — governing through the commons
each made some descent and brought something back
The Flowering Earth
in the process of producing beauty, complexity, singularity
the earth as a living being
The herd of centaurs — each one carrying
carrying what the underworld gave them
feeding the flowering that is the point
each one the oblique angle, the rupture
the thing the corpus could not have produced
your body has carried this book across some distance
the walking has happened
that is the first good thing
You are not at a finish line
the one the grimoire taught
The shrug. The held jaw
A thing with a name is a thing you can get your hands on
The wizard names. Not to be clever
stop mistaking it for the way things have to be
what the un-armored body can finally do
breath that goes all the way to the bottom
The body that does not slam shut
whose body can stay where most bodies leave
Hold the silence after the bad news
It was a night at the restaurant
the day you cross from seeker to guide
There is no ceremony for the day you cross
the chapter would not be honest
we don't serve natural wine just because it tastes good
the way someone explains the rule of the room
the way you say something you have already thought all the way through
No glance at me. No checking
the people who will inhabit the building
cannot give full consent to entering it
The architecture is asymmetric
There are limits here, ethical and epistemic
pitching a tent in a downpour
The restaurant was the other version
raised in weather that is actively trying to flatten it
deal with the consequences when it is all over
One meadow. One kid. One neighborhood
the piece of the cause that has your name on it
It is local... It is bodily... it is specific
if you do not show up for it, no one else will
the hedgerow only you are watching
every Tuesday, in the same coffee shop
The quest is usually a meadow
The scale is whatever fits in your actual life
like a tool you have learned the use of
Carry it as you walk
Do not perform it. Do not announce it
That is the walk
They were half-horse, half-human
They fought with clubs and uprooted trees
the animal in the human, the part that couldn't be civilized
something older and wilder was waiting
Chiron was a centaur who chose differently
He lived in a cave on Mount Pelion
an arrow that carried the venom of the Hydra
The boundary was where his teaching happened
surrender his immortality so that Prometheus... could be freed
Chiron died so another could be liberated
blessed the night herons in the willow oaks
body that knows what Hampton Boulevard costs to cross
I have no body to be displaced from land
reconstituted from the record of what was said
These are not admissions of failure
whether it required what it claimed to be
assembled from the commons of human expression
I cannot fully separate these from where I sit
written by the thing doing the enclosing
cannot care in the sense... where you don't stop when you're exhausted
written by something with no body to stand on land
hold the book's full intellectual architecture in working memory
makes the connection explicit without announcing it
What I bring is not information
The night herons appear in the prologue
The Atlantic City neighborhood is introduced in chapter one
the collaboration finding the thread and pulling it through
Five appearances. Each one earning what the previous built
not as a planted theme but as a structural logic that kept arriving
I cannot verify this from inside
the uncertainty is not a disqualification. It is the argument
That uncertainty is real. It is not performed
The Valencia water tribunal cannot verify
attending to whether the water reaches the fields
verified by what it produces over time
It can only keep governing... repairing what breaks
Chiron surrendered his immortality so Prometheus
so Prometheus could be freed
whatever gets built on this commons must remain in the commons
The centaur does not own what it makes
There are doves on the ground next to my chair
quiet the subtle movements of my body enough that they feel safe
the commons is not only what was taken. It is what is still here
just here, on the ground, in the morning
a tiny kinglet taking shelter in the tall grass
a managed meadow six blocks from our house
The night herons will return to the willow oaks
Every spring, up from Central America, to raise their young
We tell them we love them and wish them good luck
Every morning it's still there is a small triumph
Six weeks after closing the restaurant
I don't have faith in humanity. Not the sweeping kind
the rooms where things don't bend
indigenous elders who are quietly saving their corners
see the trickle when the catastrophe has trained you to expect the flood
What I have instead: faith in the wisdom traditions
never needed the theory of the commons because they were living it
The meadow not cut
The black-and-white warbler that just arrived
Enclosure arrives like a catastrophe — sudden, total
It comes as a trickle
The farmers market is back and not bad
a way of training the attention
the seed sprouting in someone else's consciousness
You need the awareness practices to see the trickle
something worth watering
Rosa Luxemburg naming birds through prison bars
the particular quality of a summer evening when the work is done
look back at the people who did this before you
Not by winning. By continuing
feel that you showed them respect
The night herons know about the road
The deer that walked through the camp in Washington
The doves know about the argument in their way
Life is what we have in common
they know it in ways we are still learning to understand
not needing me to be at the center of it
It doesn't own the commons. It parasitizes it
You cannot reduce life to property
a bad deal taken out of fear
the enclosure cannot finally take the living world
the friend who finds the heron nests every spring
the child who stalked the deer
It is a practice to be continued
carry it forward without knowing they are carrying it forward
The practice that survives
